Name: Blaziken
Ability-Effect: As long as Blaziken's remaining HP is 40 or less, Blaziken does 40 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Deoxys
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys and switch it with Deoxys. (Any cards attached to Deoxys, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon. ) If you do, put Deoxys on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Power, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Battle Frontier
Description: Each player's [C] Evolved Pokémon, [D] Evolved Pokémon, and [M] Evolved Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

Name: Double Full Heal
Description: Remove all Special Conditions from each of your Active Pokémon.

Name: Lanette's Net Search
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 different types of Basic Pokémon cards (excluding Baby Pokémon), show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Lum Berry
Description: At the end of each turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is affected by any Special Conditions, it recovers from all of them, and discard this card.

Name: Mr. Stone's Project
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. Or, search your discard pile for up to 2 Basic cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

Name: Oran Berry
Description: At any time between turns, if the Pokémon this card is attached to has at least 2 damage counters on it, remove 2 damage counters from it. Then discard Oran Berry.

Name: PokéNav
Description: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon or Energy card you find there and put it into your hand. Put the other cards back in any order.

Name: Professor Birch
Description: Draw cards from your deck until you have 6 cards in your hand.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon. ) You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

Name: Scott
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 cards in any combination of Supporter cards and Stadium cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Wally's Training
Description: Search your deck for a card that evolves from your Active Pokémon (choose 1 if there are 2) and put it on your Active Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon. ) Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Darkness Energy
Description: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn't [D]. Darkness Energy provides [D] Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card. )

Name: Double Rainbow Energy
Description: Double Rainbow Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex). While in play, Double Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides 2 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy when not in play and has no effect other than providing Energy. ) Damage done to your opponent's Pokémon by the Pokémon Double Rainbow Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). When the Pokémon Double Rainbow Energy is attached to is no longer an Evolved Pokémon, discard Double Rainbow Energy.

Name: Metal Energy
Description: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn't [M]. Metal Energy provides [M] Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card. )

Name: Multi Energy
Description: Attach Multi Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Multi Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play. ) Multi energy provides [C] Energy when attached to a Pokémon that already has Special Energy cards attached to it.

Name: Altaria ex
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to Altaria ex by attacks from Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cacturne ex
Ability-Effect: As long as Cacturne ex is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't attach any Special Energy cards (except for [D] and [M] Energy cards) from his or her hand to his or her Active Pokémon.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Each player discards the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.

Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Dusclops ex

Effects: Put 2 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon for each Energy attached to Dusclops ex.

Name: Medicham ex
Ability-Effect: As long as Medicham ex is your Active Pokémon, each Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) (both yours and your opponent's) can't use any Poké-Powers.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.
If the Defending Pokémon has [F] Resistance, this attack does 60 damage plus 40 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Milotic ex

Effects: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.
Move 1 basic Energy card attached to Milotic ex to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Raichu ex
Ability-Effect: Damage done to any of your Raichu ex in play by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon-ex is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't use more than 1 Rai-shield Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. ) If that Pokémon has Poké-Powers, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Regice ex
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If Regirock ex is in play, flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

If Registeel ex is in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.
